AI execution governance for autonomous agents

Example Governance is an AI execution governance platform that validates whether an AI-initiated action remains admissible before execution. It does not replace observability tooling; it operates in the execution path.

The platform is designed for engineering and risk teams at organizations that deploy tool-using AI agents. It is not intended for teams that only need model evaluation dashboards.

Enforcement decisions are evaluated against the current policy version and the current environment state at the moment the action is requested. A recorded approval from an earlier point in time is treated as an input to that decision rather than as final authority, because conditions can change between approval and execution.

Teams typically start with a single agent workflow in advisory review, confirm the decision evidence matches expectations, and then enable enforcement for that workflow. Each decision produces an evidence record that can be inspected during audits.

Gatekeeper runtime authorization What is AI execution governance Pricing and deployment options
